headline: Transcript: Gold, silver and crypto
summary: Katie Martin and Robert Armstrong discuss what’s driving the run-up in substitutes to fiat currency
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Unhedged podcast episode: ‘Gold, silver and crypto’

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Katie MartinThis has been a big year for shiny stuff. Gold, of course, has had a spectacular run higher, cruising to $4,000 an ounce and beyond. But stand aside, gold, because your scrappy little cousin, silver, would like a word. What a year it has been for silver. So gold’s up like 60-ish per cent, silver’s up a 120 per cent this year.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Silver fever, boys and girls get on board. So is the rise in the price of silver a sign of fear in markets or a sign that bubbly exuberance is taking hold. And what does this all have to do with gold? And also, I hate to say it, but with bitcoin. Today on the show, fear, greed and slightly wacky paranoid world views.

This is Unhedged, the markets and finance podcast from the Financial Times and Pushkin. I’m Katie Martin, a markets economist here at the FT and very Christmassy London. Have some hard yards of Christmas drinks ahead
